response.justification: Mohammed has strong distributed systems experience, deep full-stack expertise, and clear ownership mindset through startup and open-source leadership, but lacks explicit evidence of AI-native engineering or agentic code production experience — his AI-agent infrastructure work is mentioned briefly without depth, and his primary stack leans toward TypeScript/Node.js with DLang, not Python, raising concerns about Python mastery and alignment with the role's AI-driven code review core.
strengths: Proven experience in high-throughput, distributed systems (e.g., millions of requests/sec at Dunnhumby)
Strong ownership and full-stack initiative across startups, open-source, and technical leadership roles
Active contributor to developer tooling and infrastructure (e.g., GISCollective, Fluent-Asserts, CI/CD setups)
concerns: No clear demonstration of Python mastery — primary language exposure is TypeScript/Node.js and DLang
AI-agent infrastructure mention is superficial; no evidence of reviewing/extending AI-generated code as core responsibility
Lacks explicit agile scrum champion or on-call incident response documentation despite operational claims
